rationale: US-055 adds a new compositable overlay system that sits alongside (not inside) the existing GeometrySystem carousel. It introduces a new spatial-indexing utility, two new shaders, and extends three existing interfaces (LoopDeps, QualityProfile, GeometrySystem). The overlay pattern is novel for this codebase — all current visual systems are GeometrySystem implementations managed by ModeManager — but the plan's callback-based design keeps coupling low. No existing rendering contracts are broken.

files_modified.change: Add maxConstellationLines field to QualityProfile interface and all three tier return values
Add optional getMesh?() method to GeometrySystem interface
files_modified.reason: Optional method — no existing implementations need to change, though systems that want constellation lines must implement it

files_modified.change: Add constellationOverlay, getActiveIndex, getTransitioning to LoopDeps; add per-frame constellation draw call after geometry draw; pass bass energy and transition state to overlay
files_modified.reason: renderLoop is the critical frame path. Adding a second draw call introduces a new performance-sensitive code path. The LoopDeps interface grows by 3 optional fields.

files_modified.change: Import and instantiate ConstellationOverlay, wire getActivePositions/getActiveMesh callbacks, create systems array reference, assign new LoopDeps fields
files_modified.reason: main.ts is already the largest orchestration file. Adding constellation wiring increases its complexity. The systems array must match modeManager factory order — a subtle ordering contract.

files_modified.change: Add positions getter (Float32Array of base positions) and getMesh() returning the Points mesh
files_modified.reason: Read-only accessors on existing data structures

files_modified.change: Add positions getter from BufferGeometry attribute (not from 2D Particle model) and getMesh()
files_modified.reason: ParticleField uses a 2D Particle model internally. The getter must read from the GPU BufferAttribute to get correct 3D positions, which is a non-obvious indirection

files_created.purpose: Grid-based spatial index for efficient proximity pair discovery. cellSize must equal maxThreshold.
files_created.reason: New algorithmic code — correctness of cell traversal (27-neighbor search) directly affects visual output and CPU cost

files_created.purpose: Factory creating the overlay: LineSegments mesh, rebuild() for pair computation, draw() for per-frame buffer updates
files_created.reason: Most complex new file. Manages its own BufferGeometry lifecycle, caches pairs at rebuild, filters by bass-modulated threshold per frame, and must handle mode transitions (opacity 0 during crossfade). Incorrect lifecycle management could leak GPU resources.

files_created.purpose: Vertex shader passing depth to fragment for fog calculation
Fragment shader with distance-based opacity fade and depth fog matching existing fog convention
files_created.reason: Fog pattern already validated across 4 existing fragment shaders

new_patterns_introduced.pattern: Overlay system composited over GeometrySystem carousel
new_patterns_introduced.description: ConstellationOverlay is the first visual element that is NOT a GeometrySystem managed by ModeManager. It runs parallel to the carousel, reading positions from the active system via callbacks. This establishes a precedent for future overlay-style effects.
new_patterns_introduced.concern: If more overlays follow this pattern, renderLoop will accumulate ad-hoc draw calls. Consider a composable overlay registry if a third overlay is ever needed.

new_patterns_introduced.description: getActivePositions() and getActiveMesh() callbacks in main.ts bridge the constellation overlay to the active geometry system without the overlay knowing which system is active.
new_patterns_introduced.concern: The systems array in main.ts must maintain order parity with the modeManager factories list — an implicit contract that could break silently if modes are reordered.

new_patterns_introduced.pattern: CPU-side spatial indexing with cached pairs
new_patterns_introduced.description: SpatialHash pre-computes all pairs at maxThreshold during rebuild(), then filters per-frame by effective threshold. This is a new CPU-side computation pattern not present in the existing per-frame GPU-only pipeline.
new_patterns_introduced.concern: rebuild() is called on every mode transition. For 2000 points with 27-cell neighbor search, this is O(N * 27 * avg_cell_occupancy). Should complete in <5ms but is worth profiling.

performance_considerations.impact: Each frame filters cached pairs and writes surviving endpoints to a Float32Array + opacity array. For high tier (up to ~500 lines = 3000 floats), this is a modest CPU cost plus a bufferSubData upload.
performance_considerations.mitigation: Quality tier caps maxConstellationLines. Low tier should disable entirely or cap at ~30 lines.

performance_considerations.impact: Adds one additional draw call per frame (LineSegments). Current pipeline has 1-2 draw calls (active system + optional transitioning system).
performance_considerations.mitigation: Single draw call via LineSegments with drawRange — negligible GPU overhead for line primitives.

performance_considerations.impact: Spatial hash construction + pair finding runs synchronously when the active mode changes (~every 90-180s). Could cause a frame spike.
performance_considerations.mitigation: Could defer rebuild to next idle frame or spread across 2 frames if profiling shows >8ms. Unlikely to be noticeable at transition frequency.

quality_tier_impact.low: Constellation lines should be disabled entirely (maxConstellationLines: 0). Low-tier devices already run at 200 points — spatial hash overhead is minimal but the extra draw call and buffer writes are unnecessary.
quality_tier_impact.medium: Cap at ~100-150 lines. Provides visible constellation effect without significant GPU/CPU cost.
quality_tier_impact.high: Cap at ~300-500 lines. Full constellation effect with bass-modulated threshold range.

dependency_chain_validation.US-017: Point cloud system — provides positions getter. Required for constellation to read point positions.
dependency_chain_validation.US-040: Mode manager — provides activeIndex and transitioning state. Required for constellation to track which system's positions to use and when to hide during transitions.
dependency_chain_validation.US-046: Quality tier system — provides QualityProfile. Required for maxConstellationLines config.
dependency_chain_validation.US-047: Shader infrastructure — provides shader import conventions and error collection. Required for new GLSL files.

risks.description: GPU resource leak if constellation overlay is not properly cleaned up on mode transitions. The overlay creates its own BufferGeometry and ShaderMaterial that persist across the ModeManager lifecycle — they are not managed by ModeManager's cleanup path.
risks.mitigation: ConstellationOverlay must have explicit dispose() called on page unload. Since it persists across mode transitions (only rebuilding its line buffer), this is less of a leak risk than it first appears — but the geometry/material must still be disposable.

risks.description: Position data staleness: constellation reads base positions from the active system, but GPU-side vertex shaders apply bass deformation, twist, and jitter. The constellation lines connect CPU-side positions that don't match the visually deformed GPU positions.
risks.mitigation: This is an acceptable artistic compromise noted in the plan. Lines will approximately connect points but won't track per-frame GPU deformation. The visual effect still reads as constellation-like because the base topology is correct.

risks.description: Systems array ordering contract: main.ts must maintain a systems[] array whose indices match the modeManager factory registration order. This is an implicit, undocumented coupling.
risks.mitigation: Add a comment in main.ts documenting the ordering contract. Alternatively, expose a getActiveSystem() method on ModeManager to eliminate the need for index-based lookup entirely.

verdict_rationale: The plan is architecturally sound. It introduces a well-scoped overlay pattern that composes cleanly with the existing GeometrySystem carousel without modifying the core rendering contract. All interface changes are additive (optional fields/methods). The review.json corrections (cached pairs, type-safe LoopDeps, BufferAttribute positions) address the main implementation risks. The two medium-severity risks (position staleness, ordering contract) are acceptable trade-offs. No existing tests or contracts are broken.
